features: TL-UL CSR read/write access with byte-addressed 32-bit registers
AES-128 CBC encrypt/decrypt functional path with IV handling
128-bit key programming via four consecutive CSR writes
128-bit data input programming via four consecutive CSR writes
128-bit data output readback via four consecutive CSR reads
Start trigger sequencing and operation completion interrupt
Status polling for output_valid and input_ready behavior
Reset behavior and CSR default state
Alert output sanity and fatal-error observation
TL-UL protocol compliance on basic legal transactions
Register access permissions and address map sanity
AES round-trip correctness against a software reference model

directed_tests.name: smoke_reset_and_csr_map
directed_tests.intent: Verify reset deassertion, TL-UL connectivity, readable/writable CSR locations, WO/RW access behavior, and that reserved/undefined addresses do not crash the DUT.

directed_tests.name: aes_ecb_encrypt_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program a known AES-128 key and plaintext, start ECB encryption, and compare ciphertext against a software reference model.

directed_tests.name: aes_ecb_decrypt_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program a known AES-128 key and ciphertext, start ECB decryption, and compare recovered plaintext against the reference model.

directed_tests.name: aes_cbc_encrypt_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program key, IV, and plaintext for CBC encryption, verify ciphertext and IV update behavior against the reference model.

directed_tests.name: aes_cbc_decrypt_known_vector
directed_tests.intent: Program key, IV, and ciphertext for CBC decryption, verify plaintext recovery and IV update behavior against the reference model.

directed_tests.name: back_to_back_block_processing
directed_tests.intent: Issue consecutive start triggers with new input data and confirm the DUT accepts the next block when input_ready indicates availability, preserving output ordering.

directed_tests.name: clear_controls_behavior
directed_tests.intent: Exercise key_iv_data_in_clear and data_out_clear separately, confirming registers are zeroized and that clear operations do not corrupt unrelated control/status state.

directed_tests.name: interrupt_completion_flow
directed_tests.intent: Verify intr_o asserts on operation completion and that status/output readback can be used to clear or acknowledge the completed transaction in the expected software flow.

random_tests.name: tlul_csr_fuzz_legal_ops
random_tests.intent: Run constrained-random TL-UL reads and writes over valid CSR addresses, checking protocol stability, access permissions, and no unexpected alerting on legal traffic.

random_tests.name: aes_mode_op_randomized
random_tests.intent: Randomize mode, encrypt/decrypt selection, key, IV, and data blocks across ECB/CBC operations and compare results to a Python AES reference model.

random_tests.name: multi_block_cbc_stream
random_tests.intent: Generate short random CBC sequences of 2 to 4 blocks to stress IV chaining, output overwrite timing, and back-to-back operation handling.

random_tests.name: reset_interruption_stress
random_tests.intent: Assert reset during idle and during active operation at random times to confirm safe recovery, CSR reinitialization, and no stuck interrupt or alert conditions.

risk_areas.reason: Primary functional risk because the DUT implements a full AES-128 datapath with multiple rounds and key expansion; a single transformation bug breaks all crypto results.

risk_areas.area: CBC IV chaining and update semantics
risk_areas.reason: CBC introduces stateful behavior across blocks; incorrect IV update or first-block handling will only appear in multi-block scenarios.
Throughput-oriented control flow can expose race conditions between input_ready, start trigger, and output overwrite behavior.
128-bit values are split across four 32-bit registers and include WO/RW distinctions; address or ordering mistakes are common integration bugs.
The spec notes undefined interaction when clear and start are asserted together, so separate clear-path validation is important while avoiding ambiguous combinations.
Software flow depends on intr_o, output_valid, and input_ready; mismatches can make the block appear hung even if datapath is correct.
Alert is likely tied to fatal internal conditions, but with limited time it should be sanity-checked rather than exhaustively fault-injected.
